title: containers fail to start in rawhide
body:
### Issue Description

My toolboxes fail to start in rawhide.

podman-5.7.0~rc1-1.fc44.x86_64
toolbox-0.3-2.fc44.x86_64

comments.body:
@matthiasclasen Please provide the full info as requested, we always need `podman info`.

Did it used to work for you or did you just used it for the first time? I assume it is a regression. If so did it start with the podman 5.7-rc1 update? Can you downgrade podman to confirm? Did you reboot to see if it makes a difference?
Also can please provide a full reproducer? Does it happen if you create a new toolbox (maybe try one based on another image)

Also did you try to run a normal non toolbox container?

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).
`podman info` results might also be helpful.

My understanding based on trying to debug it with him is:

> Does it happen if you create a new toolbox (maybe try one based on another image)

Yes, a newly created Toolbx container did work.

> Also did you try to run a normal non toolbox container?

This had worked:
```
$ podman run --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:44 echo "hello world"
hello world
```

Still broken in todays rawhide.

As the rishi said, creating a new toolbox works fine, but my preexisting ones don't start anymore.

Here is the output of podman info:


Did you verify that this is problem is indeed caused by 5.7? Can you downgrade and test please? If it works then building podman from source and doing git bisect would be best so we at least know where to look. If it also doesn't work on 5.6 then we know it is not caused by podman most likely.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Without reproducer steps or some bisect commit we cannot help, sorry
